title: PRD: BrokMail production email client
description:
Create and execute the BrokMail production PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/brokmail-prd.md

Scope:
- Real Gmail and Google Calendar through Composio popup flows.
- Pi-powered concise mail assistant using live context.
- Inbox, needs-reply, follow-ups, drafts, sent, newsletters, receipts, calendar, and automations views.
- Approval-gated draft, archive, create calendar, and delete calendar actions.
- Production telemetry for connector status, fetch latency, assistant latency, approvals, and action results.

Acceptance:
- Signed-in allowlisted user opens /brokmail.
- Gmail popup connection returns to BrokMail and loads real threads.
- Calendar popup connection returns and loads real events.
- Triage, search, summarize, and draft flows use live context.
- Draft/archive/calendar writes require signed, one-use approval.
- Errors are human-readable and never raw provider dumps.

title: PRD: BrokCode Lovable-style cloud builder and TUI
description:
Create and execute the BrokCode production PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/brokcode-prd.md

Scope:
- Chat-left, preview-right browser builder.
- Durable projects, files, versions, session sync, preview URLs, deploy URLs.
- Pi coding-agent runtime with explicit unavailable state.
- Hot preview, one-click deploy, Brok/Railway subdomain path.
- Composio GitHub connection, repo context, and approval-gated PR creation.
- Local TUI sync with BROKCODE_SESSION_ID.

Acceptance:
- Signed-in user opens /brokcode without pasting a browser API key.
- Prompt creates a project, files, execution run, and preview status.
- Preview loads or shows precise runtime failure.
- Deploy creates reachable URL and stores it on project/version.
- TUI /usage works against /api/v1/usage.
- GitHub PR flow works only after approval.
- Usage dashboards show BrokCode endpoint/runtime telemetry.

title: PRD: Composio integrations platform
description:
Create and execute the integrations PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/integrations-prd.md

Scope:
- Composio popup connection model.
- Featured toolkits: Google Super, Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Docs, Google Meet, GitHub, Linear, Supabase, Slack.
- Generic connect/status routes with authenticated Brok users.
- Clear ready/connected/unavailable/error states.
- Product surfaces can open connector popups from chat/actions.

Acceptance:
- /integrations shows configured toolkit status.
- Connect opens popup and returns to Brok.
- Status routes return deterministic JSON.
- Failed Composio calls produce concise setup/reconnect messages.
- No anonymous fallback IDs are used in production.

title: PRD: Discover, Library, and Spaces knowledge system
description:
Create and execute the Discover/Library/Spaces PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/discover-library-spaces-prd.md

Scope:
- Saved threads, citations, uploaded files, background tasks, and product activity.
- Discover feed from actual workspace data.
- Library with saved threads, source domains, files, public/share state, and collections.
- Spaces grouped into Research, BrokMail, BrokCode, and API Platform.
- Long task ledger that survives tab closure.

Acceptance:
- /discover, /library, /spaces open for signed-in users.
- Recent saved chats and sources appear from real data.
- File counts appear after upload.
- Active background tasks are visible while queued/running.
- Thread links reopen the matching /search/[id].
- RLS/user scoping prevents cross-user leakage.

title: PRD: Brok API, usage, billing, and admin control plane
description:
Create and execute the Brok API platform PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/platform-api-usage-billing-prd.md

Scope:
- API key creation, hashing, scopes, allowed models, RPM, daily limits, monthly budgets.
- OpenAI-compatible chat, search completions, Anthropic messages, models, and usage routes.
- Usage, billing, and admin dashboards with real ledger data.
- Admin allowlist, provider routing, logs, cost, latency, and failure telemetry.
- Playground and docs that match actual route behavior.

Acceptance:
- User creates a key and calls chat/search/models/usage.
- Scope, model, rate, daily, and monthly budget enforcement works.
- Usage dashboard updates after traffic.
- Admin dashboard shows allowlist and real usage.
- Playground streams with a valid key.
- Docs examples match current API behavior.

title: PRD: Brok Tools, AI Humanizer, and feature request widget
description:
Create and execute the Brok Tools PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/tools-and-subproducts-prd.md

Scope:
- Production tools index.
- AI Humanizer with pattern detection, rewrite, and optional voice calibration.
- Fixed bottom-right feature request widget that collapses to a question mark and expands on hover.
- Admin queue for account-linked feature requests.
- Route metadata and safe server-rendered discoverability.

Acceptance:
- Signed-in user opens /tools and /tools/humanizer.
- Humanizer rewrites text and shows useful pattern audit.
- Voice sample changes output style.
- Feature request submissions include account identity and appear in admin.
- Route metadata exists and mobile layout is usable.

title: PRD: Integrate InsForge as BrokCode backend provider
description:
Create and execute the InsForge + BrokCode backend PRD.

Source doc: docs/prd/insforge-brokcode-backend-prd.md

Scope:
- Add InsForge as a first-class BrokCode backend provider.
- Support new InsForge cloud/trial, existing InsForge, and self-hosted InsForge modes.
- Store InsForge admin credentials server-side only.
- Surface backend URL, dashboard URL, claim URL, provisioning status, health, and capabilities in BrokCode.
- Use MiniMax for BrokCode planning while InsForge supplies backend primitives.
- Generate full-stack apps using InsForge auth, database, storage, functions, realtime, and deployment where available.

Acceptance:
- Signed-in user can create a BrokCode project with InsForge selected.
- BrokCode securely stores backend metadata and associates it with the project/workspace.
- BrokCode polls and displays InsForge health and links.
- MiniMax-powered run can generate a simple full-stack app using InsForge auth/database/storage.
- Generated frontend receives only public InsForge config.
- Errors are precise and never leak secret keys.
